s2.ptxt: Clean the contact surface between the axle hub and a new skid control sensor.

atten3.ptxt: Prevent foreign matter from attaching to the sensor rotor.

s2.ptxt: Place the skid control sensor on the axle hub so that the connector is positioned as shown in the illustration.
Using SST, a press, 2 V-blocks, and 2 steel plates, install the skid control sensor to the axle hub.

item.ptxt: Keep the sensor away from magnets.
Do not use a hammer on the skid control sensor.
Check that there is no foreign matter such as iron chips on the detecting portion of the skid control sensor.
Slowly press the skid control sensor straight.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR AXLE HUB AND BEARING ASSEMBLY WITH SKID CONTROL SENSOR

s2.ptxt: Install the hub and bearing assembly with the 4 bolts.
Connect the skid control sensor connector.

atten3.ptxt: Do not twist the sensor wire when connecting it.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT REAR AXLE HUB BEARING LOOSENESS

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, check for looseness near the center of the axle hub.

atten3.ptxt: Ensure that the dial indicator is set perpendicular to the measurement surface.

s2.ptxt: If looseness exceeds the maximum, replace the axle hub assembly.
Using a dial indicator, check for runout on the surface of the axle hub outside the hub bolt.

atten3.ptxt: Ensure that the dial indicator is set perpendicular to the measurement surface.

s2.ptxt: If runout exceeds the maximum, replace the axle hub assembly.
Aligning the matchmarks, install the rear disc.

atten4.ptxt: When replacing the rear disc with a new one, select the installation position where the rear disc has the minimum runout.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL REAR DISC BRAKE CALIPER ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the rear disc brake caliper with the 2 bolts.

atten3.ptxt: Do not twist the brake hose when installing the rear disc brake caliper assembly LH.

s2.ptxt: Install the rear flexible hose with the bolt.
Connect the connector to the skid control sensor.

s-1.ptxt: INSPECT AND ADJUST REAR WHEEL ALIGNMENT
